REVIEWED_FILES_COUNT: 18
REVIEWED_FILES: README.md | testforge/README.md | testforge/docs/HOST-COMPATIBILITY.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CODEX.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CLAUDE.md | testforge/docs/QUICK-START.md | testforge/docs/WORKFLOWS.md | testforge/docs/CAPABILITY-MATRIX.md | testforge/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md | testforge/docs/DATA-AND-PRIVACY.md | testforge/docs/TERMS-OF-USE.md | testforge/SECURITY.md | testforge/docs/VALIDATION.md | testforge/docs/LIMITATIONS.md | testforge/docs/SUPPORT-AND-VERSIONING.md | RELEASE-NOTES-v1.1.1.md | ARCHIVE-CUSTODY.md | testforge/PROVENANCE.md
MATERIAL_DOCUMENTATION_FINDINGS_COUNT: 0
ALL_REQUIRED_CUSTOMER_MOMENTS_AND_SAFETY_BOUNDARIES_INSPECTED: yes
PASS_EVIDENCE_ORIENTATION: README.md | testforge/README.md :: README.md and testforge/README.md introduce TestForge as a free, open‑source verification Augment, outline its core risk‑ranking workflow, detail licensing under MIT and CC BY‑ND 4.0, and provide clear entry points for Codex and Claude users.
PASS_EVIDENCE_INSTALLATION: testforge/docs/HOST-COMPATIBILITY.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CODEX.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CLAUDE.md :: The installation documentation explains how to deploy TestForge on Codex via the marketplace and as a standalone skill, details required file structure, verifies discovery steps, and outlines Claude upload procedures with explicit ZIP names, ensuring users can install correctly.
PASS_EVIDENCE_FIRST_VALUE: testforge/docs/QUICK-START.md :: Quick‑Start provides step‑by‑step instructions for installing TestForge, running an initial verification on a sample repository, and executing the independent reviewer, delivering the first tangible evidence package that demonstrates core functionality.
PASS_EVIDENCE_NORMAL_USE: testforge/docs/WORKFLOWS.md | testforge/docs/CAPABILITY-MATRIX.md :: Workflows and Capability Matrix documents describe the full verification lifecycle, including impact mapping, risk ranking, test execution, evidence collection, reviewer challenge, and release decision steps, covering all normal operational scenarios.
PASS_EVIDENCE_RECOVERY: testforge/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md :: Troubleshooting offers clear guidance for common issues such as unknown stack detection, missing YAML dependencies, execution failures, and path resolution problems, enabling users to recover from typical installation or runtime errors.
PASS_EVIDENCE_PRIVACY_SECURITY: testforge/docs/DATA-AND-PRIVACY.md | testforge/docs/TERMS-OF-USE.md | testforge/SECURITY.md :: Data and privacy, terms of use, and security documents collectively assert that TestForge operates locally without telemetry, disallows destructive actions by default, requires explicit human authorization for sensitive operations, and provides clear reporting channels for vulnerabilities.
PASS_EVIDENCE_EVIDENCE_LIMITS: testforge/docs/VALIDATION.md | testforge/docs/LIMITATIONS.md :: Validation and limitations sections clarify that TestForge’s evidence is limited to executed tests, deterministic scripts, and behavioral evaluations; it does not guarantee defect freedom or compliance, and acknowledges untested paths remain unknown.
PASS_EVIDENCE_SUPPORT_MAINTENANCE: testforge/docs/SUPPORT-AND-VERSIONING.md | RELEASE-NOTES-v1.1.1.md | ARCHIVE-CUSTODY.md :: Support and versioning, release notes, and archive custody documents provide transparent change logs, semantic versioning guidance, issue‑tracker links, and detailed artifact custody records, ensuring users can track updates and recover previous releases.
PASS_EVIDENCE_PROVENANCE: testforge/PROVENANCE.md :: The provenance file documents the build history, versioning milestones, and source‑control lineage of TestForge v1.1.1, confirming that all artifacts originate from approved Augment maps and promptcraft guidance.

REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS

- In INSTALL-CLAUDE.md, instructions mention uploading claude-ai/software-verification-v1.1.1.zip etc. Good.
